headline: Gap Encore Loyalty Links Apparel Spending With Entertainment Partnerships
summary: Gap (NYSE:GAP) has introduced a new loyalty program called "Encore". The program connects Gap's brands with entertainment partners Disney, NBCUniversal, and AMC Theaters. The launch marks a shift in how Gap aims to engage customers across retail and entertainment channels. Gap operates a portfolio of apparel brands, and Encore adds another layer to how the company tries to keep customers coming back across its stores and online channels. By tying rewards to entertainment names like Disney,...

headline: 'We're Lucky Because We Have Our Own TPUs,' Says DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis — Yet Admits AI Memory Shortage Constrains Research
summary: The global artificial intelligence race is accelerating, but even the most advanced labs are running into hardware limits. Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google's DeepMind AI unit, recently told CNBC that shortages in memory, graphics processing units and electricity are slowing AI deployment. "Yes, I think that’s constraining a lot of the deployment for sure," Hassabis said. China Closing The Gap China remains central to the global AI competition. headline: Is It Time To Reconsider Disney (DIS) After A 46% Five Year Share Price Slide
summary: If you are wondering whether Walt Disney's current share price reflects its true worth, you are not alone. This article looks at what the numbers say about value. At a last close of US$106.04, the stock has a 1.6% gain over the past 7 days, while returns are a 6.0% decline over 30 days, a 5.2% decline year to date, and a 4.9% decline over 1 year, set against a 7.8% gain over 3 years and a 46.2% decline over 5 years. These mixed returns sit alongside ongoing investor focus on how Disney...

headline: Netflix boss: I carry anxiety about the outcome and where we landed
summary: The battle for control of Warner Bros Discovery is over. In one of the biggest entertainment deals in history, the maker of the Harry Potter franchise and every extraordinary HBO drama series from The Sopranos to Succession was sold at auction on Thursday for $111bn (£82bn).

content.title: Netflix boss: I carry anxiety about the outcome and where we landed
content.summary: The battle for control of Warner Bros Discovery is over. In one of the biggest entertainment deals in history, the maker of the Harry Potter franchise and every extraordinary HBO drama series from The Sopranos to Succession was sold at auction on Thursday for $111bn (£82bn).
